cards.text: What are the anchors that have kept you two tethered to each other through everything so far?

What does a boundary mean to you?

Do you feel like you two genuinely respect each other's boundaries?
cards.context_beat_copy:
Something worth knowing before you go further:

A boundary is a limit you set for yourself, not a rule you can set for someone else.

"I won't sleep with anyone without a condom" is a boundary. It's yours. Your partner is still free to make their own choice. Now you both know where you actually stand.
cards.back_copy:
If either of you answered no to the second question, you just found your starting point.

What specific boundary has felt ignored? Name it out loud before the next card.

cards.text:
Where does communication between you two usually fall apart?

What does that moment typically look like?

Exploring this will bring things to the surface that have been easy to ignore until now.

What insecurities do you expect to show up for you?

How do you want your partner to show up when they do?

What tends to trigger it in you specifically?

What do you need to build to navigate it well in this?

In most NM spaces men find the dating landscape harder to navigate than expected.

Fewer matches. More skepticism. A quiet question that can creep in...

Am I enough?

How do you each sit with that reality?

What does it make you want to do for each other?

Women in NM often find themselves with more access than they anticipated.

That can feel electric but it can also complicate things. Enjoying all of this newness will require balance.

How do you both intend to find that balance?

What feels exciting? What feels scary?

Expectations rarely match reality.

What are you each expecting this to look and feel like?

Which of those feel solid and which ones may require thinking about more deeply?

What do you love most about your sex life together?

What's one thing you'd change, add, or explore if you could?
What excites you most about where this could go?

In most NM spaces, one of you may find the outside landscape slower than expected.

Fewer sparks. More waiting. A quiet question that can creep in...

Am I enough?

If that turns out to be you, how do you want to be met?

What do you want to promise each other before anyone finds out which way it goes?

And one of you may find more attention than you anticipated.

That can feel electric, but it can also complicate things. Enjoying the newness will take balance.

If that turns out to be you, how do you intend to find that balance?

What feels exciting? What feels scary?
